response.justification: The candidate is an accomplished senior full-stack engineer with a long track record in web, TypeScript/Node.js, and DLang, but the profile fundamentally mismatches this role. The position requires native-level Japanese, strong C for UNIX, Linux kernel source-modification experience, and embedded LAN-switch security domain knowledge (MACsec, 802.1X, L2/L3) — none of which appear on the resume. His C/C++ exposure is brief and dated (a seven-month stint in 2014-2015), and his listed languages are English, Romanian, and German, which fails a hard requirement outright.
strengths: 18+ years of broad software engineering experience across web, mobile, desktop, and some embedded-adjacent work
Low-latency systems experience (async I/O, fibers, high-throughput services in DLang) and solid Linux/Docker/CI background
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, testing tools authorship, workshops, and open-source maintenance
concerns: No Japanese language proficiency listed — the role requires native-level Japanese, a hard disqualifier
No experience with embedded networking security (MACsec, 802.1X, switch firmware) or Linux kernel modification
C programming experience is minimal and roughly a decade old; recent work is entirely web/full-stack

key_factors.factor: No embedded LAN-switch / network-security domain experience
Insufficient recent C/UNIX and Linux kernel depth
